target.name: Roam Robotics
target.founder: Tim Swift, Mallory Ulaszek, Saul Griffith

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Roam Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Tim Swift, Mallory Ulaszek, and Saul Griffith that has raised $16.4M to date. The sector comprises 9 peers total—1 public and 8 private—including companies such as Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to constraints in the peer dataset. Stage-Progression methodology, using the $16.4M total raised as a value proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yields an implied valuation of $33M. A Peer-Median approach was not applicable because the robotics and manufacturing sector contained insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum sample thresholds (fewer than three datapoints in any filtered subset), making broad-sector median comparison unreliable.

The triangulated valuation range is $28M to $38M, with a median of $33M. This range reflects moderate conservatism given the single-method foundation. Key uncertainties include the absence of meaningful same-stage peer benchmarking and reliance on a historical step-up multiplier that may not reflect current market conditions or Roam's specific competitive positioning within the robotics sector. Additional financial performance data or a narrowed peer set would materially strengthen conviction in this valuation.
